description: Adversarial: pure clinical narrative, no XML, no parenthesized codes — pure LLM domain knowledge
user:
Patient: Aaron Murphy
Gender: M
DOB: 1989-06-04
Race: White
Ethnicity: Non Hispanic or Latino
Location: Buffalo, NY 14202
Facility: Buffalo General Medical Center (NPI: 1234567815)
Encounter: 2026-07-19
This is a previously healthy 36-year-old man presenting with a 2-day history of severe sore throat, odynophagia, and subjective fevers. On exam there are bilateral tonsillar exudates, anterior cervical lymphadenopathy, and absence of cough. Rapid antigen detection test for group A Streptococcus came back positive at the point-of-care. He was started on amoxicillin 500 mg by mouth three times daily for 10 days for streptococcal pharyngitis and instructed on supportive care including acetaminophen for fever and saltwater gargles.
Vitals: Temp 38.6C, HR 92, RR 16, SpO2 99%, BP 122
